item.title: [Bloomberg] Thames Water Creditors Pitch Improved Rescue Offer to Ofwat
item.description: Thames Water’s creditors have pitched an improved rescue offer for the troubled utility that includes a bigger equity injection and an increase in committed debt, according to a person with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: [Bloomberg] Coming up on The Weekend Show | Strikes on Iran Continue
item.description: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity and context to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: [Bloomberg] Two LPG Ships Sail Through Hormuz to Shortage-Hit India
item.description: Two tankers carrying liquefied petroleum gas are heading to India after crossing the Strait of Hormuz, providing some relief to acute shortages as the war in the Persian Gulf disrupts supply of the cooking fuel.

item.title: [Bloomberg] Second Dynacom Oil Tanker Passes Hormuz as Other Owners Hold Back
item.description: A Greek shipowner sent a second oil tanker through the Strait of Hormuz, bucking caution among the shipping industry as Iran lashes out across the region in response to attacks by Israel and the US.

item.title: [Bloomberg] Iran War Supercharges Secretive Korean Tycoon’s Big Tanker Bet
item.description: As the war in Iran wreaks chaos across global energy markets, one enigmatic Korean tycoon has seen his earnings soar.

item.title: [Bloomberg] Private Credit Fears, War Darken Outlook For US Financial Stocks
item.description: Financial stocks are off to their worst start to a year since the Covid pandemic, with investors expecting more pain ahead as worries over everything from private credit to the Iran war roil the troubled sector.

item.title: [Bloomberg] Two Oil Tankers Seen at Kharg After Strikes, Satellite Firm Says
item.description: Two oil tankers were berthed at key Iranian oil-export facility Kharg Island hours after the US attacked military installations there, according to Tankertrackers.com, a firm that specializes in studying satellite imagery to follow ship movements.

item.title: [Bloomberg] Private Credit Gets ‘Yellow Alert,’ While Swedish Defense Scores Green Light
item.description: Private credit funds have turned to retail investors, who are not used to having their money tied up when things go wrong.

item.title: [Bloomberg] Trump Strikes Iran’s Kharg Oil Hub, Urges Hormuz Reopening
item.description: The US struck military sites on Kharg Island, from which Iran exports almost all its oil, for the first time overnight, upping the ante in a Middle East war that’s raged for more than two weeks and shows little sign of easing.

item.title: [Bloomberg] South Africa Mining Exploration Falls for Seventh Year
item.description: South Africa’s investment in mineral exploration dropped for a seventh consecutive year, despite the government’s ambitions to arrest the decline.

item.title: [FT] UAE port struck after US hits military assets at Iranian export hub
item.description: Authorities say Fujairah attack was intercepted and blaze under control after American forces target Kharg Island

item.title: [FT] Where are the Houthis?
item.description: Yemeni militant group is noticeably absent from Iran’s war with the US and Israel

item.title: [FT] Why Iran could become the next Iraq
item.description: The first Gulf war left a weakened regime in place without a political settlement — paving the way for future conflict

item.title: [FT] The oil price war
item.description: For an Iranian regime facing existential struggle, pushing crude up is key measure of success

item.title: [FT] Are markets being too complacent about the Iran war?
item.description: Sometimes crises do not blow over as investors might hope

item.title: [FT] Donald Trump, globalist
item.description: It should not have taken the Iran war to finish the idea that he is ‘isolationist’

item.title: [FT] Musk’s $134bn claim against OpenAI rests on ‘numbers out of the air’, judge says
item.description: California court questions billionaire’s expert witness but declines to exclude the testimony from April trial

item.title: [FT] Musk pushes out more xAI founders as AI coding effort falters
item.description: Tesla and SpaceX managers sent in to review work as billionaire’s start-up struggles to keep pace with rivals

item.title: [FT] Nvidia prepares AI ‘inference’ chip launch to counter rising challengers
item.description: Jensen Huang to unveil new products at GTC event next week as spending shifts from training to running AI models

item.title: [FT] Sovereign AI is a bet on the economies of anti-scale
item.description: Deglobalisation is expensive for individual countries, but a windfall for their suppliers
